title: Planning and Decision-Making Process: Campus-Level
description: Each campus in Conroe ISD maintains a shared decision-making team made up of teachers, parents, community members, and campus leaders who work together to guide school planning and improvement. These teams help shape decisions about instruction, school programs, and how resources are used at the campus level. Parents and community members have a meaningful role in this process, giving families a direct voice in how their local school operates.

timeline_local.summary: The board is asked to approve the 2025-2026 District Improvement Plan (DIP), as required by state law. The DIP is developed annually by the superintendent with the district-level planning and decision-making committee, then presented to the board for adoption. It guides district and campus staff in improving student performance to meet Texas Accountability System standards. This is a routine annual approval, not a change to board policy text itself.
timeline_local.discussion: policy EM (teaching about controversial issues). Raised two specific concerns: (1) the addition of "unpatriotic or anti-American sentiments" as prohibited — terms he called undefined and ambiguous; (2) a new "prohibited terms" section banning "Latinx" and "minor attracted person," which he opposed. He urged the board to give these revisions appropriate scrutiny.
timeline_local.notes: This agenda item references Board Policy BQ and BQB (Legal) as the authority for the action, but it is not a policy text amendment. It is the annual adoption of the District Improvement Plan as required by state law. Developed and approved by the District Level Planning and Decision-Making Committee prior to board presentation. Submitted by Dr. Ted Landry, Deputy Superintendent.

description_local: This policy governs how the school district creates and updates its annual District Improvement Plan, a required roadmap that outlines goals and strategies for improving student performance and overall school quality. It applies to the entire district, guiding decisions made by school leaders, staff, and the school board each year. Parents and community members should care about this policy because the improvement plan shapes priorities for how schools are run, where resources are directed, and how the district works toward better outcomes for all students.
